mission.title: Install and Configure Antrea on Kubernetes
mission.description: Antrea is a Kubernetes networking solution based on Open vSwitch, providing efficient networking and security services for Kubernetes clusters. Installing Antrea allows you to implement Kubernetes Network Policies and enhance your cluster's networking capabilities.

steps.title: Add the Antrea Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Antrea Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add antrea https://charts.antrea.io
```

steps.title: Update the Helm repository
steps.description:
Update your local Helm chart repository cache to ensure you have the latest charts:
```bash
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Antrea using Helm
steps.description:
Install Antrea in the `kube-system` namespace with the following command:
```bash
helm install antrea antrea/antrea --version 2.5.1 --namespace kube-system --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Antrea pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app=antrea
```

steps.title: Configure Antrea
steps.description:
You may want to configure resource limits for the Antrea components. Edit the Antrea deployment using:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment antrea-controller -n kube-system
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Antrea Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Antrea release with the following command:
```bash
helm uninstall antrea --namespace kube-system
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs
uninstall.description:
Remove the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) created by Antrea:
```bash
kubectl delete crd antreanetworking.antrea.io
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
If you created a dedicated namespace for Antrea, you can delete it with:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace kube-system
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and resources:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kube-system > antrea-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure your Helm repository is up to date:
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Antrea
upgrade.description:
Upgrade Antrea to the latest version with the following command:
```bash
helm upgrade antrea antrea/antrea --version 2.5.1 --namespace kube-system
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the Antrea pods are running successfully after the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app=antrea
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending
troubleshooting.description:
If you see Antrea pods stuck in Pending state, check for insufficient resources or node conditions:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n kube-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Network Policies not working
troubleshooting.description:
If Network Policies are not being enforced, ensure that the Antrea controller is running and check the logs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app=antrea-controller -n kube-system
```

troubleshooting.title: High CPU usage
troubleshooting.description:
If you notice high CPU usage by Antrea components, review the resource limits set in the deployment:
```bash
kubectl get deployment antrea-controller -n kube-system -o yaml
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Antrea will result in all Antrea pods running in the `kube-system` namespace without errors. You will be able to implement and enforce Kubernetes Network Policies effectively.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.23 or later with NodeIPAMController enabled.
